analysis: Looking at the week, you've got a real split happening here. The 9th of June was rough — you didn't get down until after midnight, had a brutal 43-minute wake somewhere around 2:30am, and the whole night was fragmented and shallow with barely any deep sleep to speak of. But then the 9th evening and the 11th both show you asleep before 9:10pm, which is ahead of your 8:30 target and honestly impressive — your body is clearly responding to something in that wind-down stack. The deep sleep blocks on those earlier nights are genuinely good, with solid 20-plus minute chunks appearing in the first cycles where they belong. The gap that keeps showing up is the late-night fragmentation, those clusters of short wakes and micro-arousals after midnight that are chopping up your REM when it should be building — worth noticing, not stressing.
